title: Cyanides - Canada.ca
content:
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). The ecological portion of the screening assessment used a moiety-based approach that focused on free cyanide [hydrogen cyanide (HCN) and the cyanide anion (CN-)] and precursors of free cyanide. As such, the ecological moiety conclusion includes, but is not limited to, the 10 substances identified in the table below; however, the human health conclusion is limited to those 10 substances.
- Summary of publications
- Information gathering Section 71 CMP 2023
CAS RN (cyanides that met categorization criteria)
Proposed order and RIAS
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Proposed risk management approach
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Meets one or more of the criteria
Refer to proposed risk management approach
Potassium dicyanoargentate
Argentate(1-), bis(cyano-C)-, potassium
Tetrasodium ferrocyanide (yellow prussiate of soda)
Ferrate(4-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, tetrasodium, ( OC -6-11)
Tripotassium ferricyanide
Ferrate(3-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, tripotassium, ( OC -6-11)-
Tetrapotassium ferrocyanide (yellow prussiate of potash)
Ferrate(4-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, tetrapotassium, ( OC -6-11)-
Potassium dicyanoaurate
Aurate(1-), bis(cyano-C)-, potassium
Ferric ferrocyanide (Prussian blue, insoluble)
Ferrate(4-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, iron(3+) (3:4), ( OC -6-11)-
Ferric ammonium ferrocyanide
Ferrate(4-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, ammonium iron(3+) (1:1:1),( OC -6-11)-
Potassium ferric ferrocyanide (Prussian blue, soluble or Turnbull's blue)
Ferrate(4-), hexakis(cyano-C)-, iron(3+) potassium (1:1:1), ( OC -6-11)-
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA 1999).
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I, Vol. 157, No. 11 and start of a 60-day public comment period on a proposed order adding free cyanide, cyanide salts and cyanide complexes to Schedule 1 to the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 .
Publication of the final screening assessment. The proposed risk management approach was also published for a 60-day public comment period. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I, Vol. 157, No. 8 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment and the risk management scope. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 6 .

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA 1999. This notice applies to HCN (CAS RN 74-90-8). More information is available on the Information gathering with respect to certain substances under the Chemicals Management Plan web page.
